item.title: Meme Aggregator 7.0
item.description:
I was in highschool while vine was popular. I loved it so much. I refuse to download tiktok. It just seems toxic?
want this as a poster
Oysters are one of my favorite foods. However, you shouldn't eat them in the warmer months. They can get this horrible bacteria that is like undetectable but can kill you???? Doesn't smell, doesnt taste, just makes you very sick and maybe die
I am married to a weirdo
Im hilarious.
Can't really read the artist signature? Vlla thynell?
Update your printer! It's hackable
Ye I've got puppies
Marie kondo
cute
I lol'd at many of these and I hope you did too
item.dc:creator: amberliketacoa

item.title: Musical dump: women of great talent edition
item.description:
Hi thank you for coming to my Sunday Funday music dump where today I'm featuring the skills of the women in the music industry. Gonna keep this one without much commentary because I already tried uploading this and this is attempt two. Please enjoy, hope you're having a good day today
Thanks again and as always, arooooo
Anyway here's unrelated Belgian techno anthem Pump Up The Jam
item.dc:creator: GravyEducation

item.title: Stop Killing Games - the EU petition just hit overdrive
item.description: Stop Destroying Games, the European Citizens' Initiative (ECI) against the planned obsolescence and destruction of video games, just hit its stretch goal. It collected 1'400'000 signatures! One million four hundred thousand! If you are an EU citizen, you can STILL SIGN it until the end of the month. https://eci.ec.europa.eu/045/public/#/screen/home This initiative is a petition that follows the steps of an official EU process to ask the European Commission to take action over an issue. Specifically, it asks for a way to allow people who bought a video game to be able to keep playing it after a publisher ends its support. Until not too many years ago, what this petition asks ask for was pretty much a given. Multiplayer games could also be played on the LAN and on private servers. These options are now missing in many new games, because publishers found a clever way to make us buy their new games, disabling the old ones. The initiative is written to allow publishers enough flexibility to implement different solutions (as long as they provide one) and it is not retroactive. BTW The title of the ECI is "Stop Destroying Games". The consumer protection movement is "Stop Killing Games". More info here https://www.stopkillinggames.com/faq Long form FAQ video here https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sEVBiN5SKuA
item.dc:creator: The11thPlague

item.title: 14ft Tiger Shark Says "Aloha" Waianae Boat Harbor
item.description: July 15, 2025 - HONOLULU (HawaiiNewsNow) - An incredible, up-close video shows the moments a massive Tiger Shark swam right into Waianae Boat Harbor. This took place just before noon on Tuesday and the big shark had quite a big crowd of onlookers. Comments on social media say the shark is a regular at the boat harbor and is known to frequent the area. #shark #awesome #hawaii #aww #mildly_interesting
item.dc:creator: OceansRust
